criteria.name: fwi_diagnostics.py exists with diagnose_chain_breaks()
criteria.evidence: src/pea_met_network/fwi_diagnostics.py exists at HEAD with ChainBreak dataclass and diagnose_chain_breaks() function.

criteria.name: diagnose_chain_breaks() correctly identifies chain breaks
criteria.evidence: Function compares null patterns of FFI codes against input dependencies using _find_null_regions(), determines cause via _determine_cause(), and produces ChainBreak objects with break_start, break_end, cause, missing_input, rows_affected.

criteria.name: _ffmc_calc, _dmc_calc, _dc_calc accept gap_threshold_hours
criteria.evidence: All three functions have gap_threshold_hours: int = 24 parameter. calculate_fwi() also accepts it and passes it through.

criteria.name: Chain restarts after gap_threshold_hours null hours
criteria.evidence: _ffmc_calc resets mo_prev from ffmc_prev when consecutive_nulls >= gap_threshold. _dmc_calc resets dmc_prev_val to dmc_prev. _dc_calc resets dc_prev_val to dc_prev.

criteria.name: Chain remains NaN for gaps below threshold (no premature restart)
criteria.evidence: _ffmc_calc correctly handles this (sets mo_prev=NaN on input NaN, checks np.isnan(mo_prev) on resume). BUT _dmc_calc and _dc_calc do NOT set their carry-over (dmc_prev_val, dc_prev_val) to NaN when inputs are missing. When inputs resume after a short gap (< threshold), these functions silently calculate from the stale pre-gap carry-over value instead of producing NaN. This violates the spec requirement that short gaps produce NaN.

criteria.name: cleaning-config.json has fwi section
criteria.evidence: docs/cleaning-config.json contains fwi.gap_threshold_hours=24 and fwi.startup_defaults with ffmc/dmc/dc.

criteria.name: fwi_missingness_report.csv generated
criteria.evidence: run_pipeline() calls chain_breaks_to_dataframe() and writes to data/processed/fwi_missingness_report.csv when chain breaks exist.

criteria.name: FWI missingness report registered in manifest
QA/QC report includes fwi_chain_breaks column
criteria.evidence: generate_qa_qc_report() accepts chain_breaks parameter and counts breaks per station as fwi_chain_breaks column.

criteria.name: pytest tests/ -m 'not e2e' -v passes
criteria.evidence: 229 passed, 1 skipped with --ignore=tests/test_cross_station_impute.py. Martin's Phase 10 TDD tests (test_cross_station_impute.py) fail to collect due to missing modules — expected TDD behavior, not a regression.

criteria.name: Greenwich FFMC coverage increases after chain recovery
criteria.evidence: Chain restart logic ensures FFMC resumes after gaps >= 24h. The 1762-hour Greenwich RH gap will trigger restart when RH returns.

criteria.name: Stanley Bridge FFMC/DMC remain 0%
criteria.evidence: Stanley Bridge has no RH sensor. _ffmc_calc and _dmc_calc both require RH as input. Since RH is never valid, the chain never restarts.

failing_nodes.message: AC-FWI-5 FAIL: _dmc_calc does not set dmc_prev_val to NaN when inputs are missing
AC-FWI-5 FAIL: _dc_calc does not set dc_prev_val to NaN when temp is missing

summary: Phase 9 chain recovery implementation is well-structured overall: fwi_diagnostics.py is clean, cleaning-config.json has the right structure, pipeline integration is correct, and QA/QC report enhancement works. However, _dmc_calc and _dc_calc have a gap-threshold enforcement bug: they don't set their carry-over state to NaN when inputs are missing, so short gaps (< 24h) silently produce values from stale carry-over instead of NaN. _ffmc_calc handles this correctly — _dmc_calc and _dc_calc need the same pattern. Martin lint has 66 violations (medium/low only) — noted but not blocking.
